title: An introduction to machine-actionable Software Management Plans - Practical steps towards FAIR4RS
author: Venkatesh, Suhasini
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Description:  This talk/tutorial introduces Software Management Plans together with a machine-actionable layer based on schema.org metadata. It shows how different bits of maSMPs align and support FAIR4RS, including practical steps towards better descriptions of research software and its corresponding metadata. This talk/tutorial reuses training materials used in other tutorials, they are indicated in the slides as a link to the original material. 
  Keywords:  Research software, metadata, FAIR4RS, Software Management Plans, machine-actionability, SMP, maSMP 
  Guiding questions:  
 
 
 Part 1 - Introduction to Software Management Plans 
 
 
 
 What is an SMP? 
 
 
 What are the benefits of SMPs? 
 
 
 Which common practices for research software are included in SMPs? 
 
 
 
 Part 2 - maSMP 
 
 
 
 What is metadata and what are its benefits? 
 
 
 How research software metadata aligns to SMPs? 
 
 
 How maSMP metadata aligns to FAIR4RS? 
 
 
 
  Learning Outcomes:  
 
 
 Describe what SMPs are and what maSMPs add to them 
 
 
 Describe what metadata including some examples of research software metadata 
 
 
 Identify common good practices supported by maSMPs aligned to FAIR4RS 
 
 
  Requirements/Prerrequisites:  
 
 
 Basic experience with research software development 
 
 
 Some knowledge of Data Management Plans could be useful 
 
 
  Additional info:  
 
  Time estimation 60 minutes 
  Level Beginner / Introductory 
  Published 2025-02-24 
  Latest modification 2025-02-24 
  License  CC-By 4.0  
  Version 1.0.0 
  Identifier DOI:10.5281/zenodo.14866849. 
  Citation Castro, L. J. (2025, February 13). An introduction to machine-actionable Software Management Plans - Practical steps towards FAIR4RS. 5th conference for Research Software Engineering in Germany (deRSE25), Karlsruhe, Germany. Zenodo.  https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.14866849 .
